title: canthium oligocarpum subsp. friesiorum
intro: Canthium oligocarpum subsp. friesiorum is a plant species also known as Fries' canthium or Fries' wild tea, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Canthium oligocarpum subsp. friesiorum include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea by infusing the leaves in hot water or as a decoction, and is also used in the form of extracts and infusions for various health purposes.
